message:
fix(claudeconfig): don't mis-delete settings.json containing JSON null

Third-pass review caught a subtle bug in removeIfEmptyObject.

json.Unmarshal of the bytes "null" into a map[string]any succeeds
(no error), but leaves the map as nil. A nil map has len() == 0,
which looked identical to a parsed empty object `{}` under the
existing `if len(parsed) != 0 { return nil }` check. Net effect:
a settings.json containing literal `null` would be deleted as if
it were `{}`.

Added an explicit `if parsed == nil { return nil }` guard ahead of
the length check. `{}` unmarshals into a non-nil zero-length map,
so it still takes the delete path; `null` (and any other JSON
content Go's unmarshaler represents as a nil map of this type)
is left alone.

Two new tests pin the distinction:
  - TestRemoveIfEmptyObjectNoOpsOnJSONNull
  - TestRemoveIfEmptyObjectNoOpsOnJSONArray (documents that `[]`
    fails to unmarshal into a map and is correctly treated as
    "non-object content, don't touch")

22 tests pass, go vet clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
